query: Nissan EV subsidy eligibility and how it applies to Nissan purchases in Japan
brand_topic_category: Value, offers, finance and total cost of ownership

request_params_without_api_key.q: Nissan EV subsidy eligibility and how it applies to Nissan purchases in Japan

search_parameters.q: Nissan EV subsidy eligibility and how it applies to Nissan purchases in Japan

text_blocks.snippet: The Japanese government provides clean energy vehicle purchase incentives through the Clean Energy Vehicle (CEV) subsidy program, administered by the Next Generation Vehicle Promotion Center (CEV-PC).

The national EV purchase subsidy caps are structured to favor automakers advancing Green Transformation (GX) metrics, local repair infrastructure, and cybersecurity. The maximum subsidy for a full-sized battery electric vehicle (BEV) sits at 1.3 million yen, while the cap for low-powered "kei" micro-EVs sits at 580,000 yen.

list.snippet: Vehicle Type: The subsidy applies only to brand-new registered vehicles. Used cars or parallel imports do not qualify.

Buyer Status: Individuals, corporations, local governments, and leasing entities holding a registered home or business address in Japan are eligible. Non-citizens can apply if they possess a valid Japanese Residence Card.

Ownership Hold Period: To prevent resale profiteering, the buyer must continuously own and operate the subsidized vehicle for a restriction period of 3 to 4 years. Disposing of, selling, or scrapping the car earlier without authorization triggers a mandatory partial or full repayment of the subsidy.

text_blocks.snippet: Application to Nissan EV Lineup in Japan

Nissan's major electric models score exceptionally well under METI's evaluation rules due to Nissan's dense domestic diagnostic infrastructure, vehicle-to-home (V2H) bidirectional charging tech, and reliable domestic supply chains.
The largest financial benefit of buying a Nissan EV in Japan comes from stacking the national CEV fund with prefectural and municipal incentives.

list.snippet: The Tokyo Advantage: If you buy a Nissan Sakura in Tokyo, the city provides an additional municipal rebate ranging from ¥450,000 to ¥750,000.

The Final Price Impact: Combining Tokyo's rebate with the national ¥580,000 Kei EV subsidy slices up to ¥1.33 million off the bill. This drops the out-of-pocket cost for a brand-new Sakura from ¥2.54 million down to roughly ¥1.21 million.
Tax Immunities: Eligible Nissan BEVs also grant the owner a 100% exemption from the environmental performance levy and the automobile weight tax at registration.

text_blocks.snippet: If you plan to purchase, your local Nissan dealer handles the initial CEV-PC digital application process automatically during the sales finalization.
If you are looking at a specific model, would you like to calculate the exact ownership tax exemptions or review the local subsidy rules for your specific prefecture in Japan?

serpapi_response.reconstructed_markdown:
The Japanese government provides clean energy vehicle purchase incentives through the Clean Energy Vehicle \(CEV\) subsidy program, administered by the [Next Generation Vehicle Promotion Center \(CEV\-PC\)](https://www.cev-pc.or.jp/english/cev-subsidy.html). [0]

The national EV purchase subsidy caps are structured to favor automakers advancing Green Transformation \(GX\) metrics, local repair infrastructure, and cybersecurity. The maximum subsidy for a full\-sized battery electric vehicle \(BEV\) sits at 1.3 million yen, while the cap for low\-powered "kei" micro\-EVs sits at 580,000 yen. [2] [17] [5] [18]

### Eligibility Criteria for Japanese Purchases

- Vehicle Type: The subsidy applies only to brand\-new registered vehicles. Used cars or parallel imports do not qualify.
- Buyer Status: Individuals, corporations, local governments, and leasing entities holding a registered home or business address in Japan are eligible. Non\-citizens can apply if they possess a valid [Japanese Residence Card](https://soukyo.co.jp/en/buy-car/).
- Ownership Hold Period: To prevent resale profiteering, the buyer must continuously own and operate the subsidized vehicle for a restriction period of 3 to 4 years. Disposing of, selling, or scrapping the car earlier without authorization triggers a mandatory partial or full [repayment of the subsidy](https://www.cev-pc.or.jp/hojo/R3ho-cev.html).

### Application to Nissan EV Lineup in Japan

Nissan's major electric models score exceptionally well under METI's evaluation rules due to Nissan's dense domestic diagnostic infrastructure, vehicle\-to\-home \(V2H\) bidirectional charging tech, and reliable domestic supply chains. [17] [3] [8] [19]

| Nissan Model | Category | Base Price \(Approx.\) | Expected National Subsidy Amount |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Nissan Sakura | Kei EV \(Micro\-car\) | ¥2.54 Million | ¥580,000 \(Full Category Cap\) |
| Nissan Leaf | Standard BEV | ¥4.1 Million\+ | ¥850,000 to ¥1.3 Million \(Based on battery capacity/trim\) |
| Nissan Ariya | Premium SUV BEV | ¥6.67 Million | ¥850,000 to ¥1.3 Million \(Based on battery capacity/trim\) |
[3] [5] [0] [2] [18] [12] [13]

### Stackability with Regional Subsidies

The largest financial benefit of buying a Nissan EV in Japan comes from stacking the national CEV fund with prefectural and municipal incentives. [3] [18]

- The Tokyo Advantage: If you buy a Nissan Sakura in Tokyo, the city provides an additional municipal rebate ranging from ¥450,000 to ¥750,000.
- The Final Price Impact: Combining Tokyo's rebate with the national ¥580,000 Kei EV subsidy slices up to ¥1.33 million off the bill. This drops the out\-of\-pocket cost for a brand\-new Sakura from ¥2.54 million down to roughly ¥1.21 million.
- Tax Immunities: Eligible Nissan BEVs also grant the owner a 100% exemption from the environmental performance levy and the automobile weight tax at registration.

If you plan to purchase, your local Nissan dealer handles the initial CEV\-PC digital application process automatically during the sales finalization.

If you are looking at a specific model, would you like to calculate the exact ownership tax exemptions or review the local subsidy rules for your specific prefecture in Japan?
